sourceMovie.title: Doctor Zhivago
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Doctor Zhivago (1965) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Suite Française
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doctor Zhivago clicked for you, Suite Française (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Doctor Zhivago?
faq.answer: Try Suite Française, Wings, Pride & Prejudice—each shares DNA with Doctor Zhivago while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Doctor Zhivago?
faq.answer: Doctor Zhivago (1965) is best described as Drama, Romance, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Doctor Zhivago worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
